text: Try it and watch it fall through to a password prompt: `ssh -i ~/deploy_key deploy@localhost`. The key is being ignored. Ask sshd why — the reason is logged: `sudo tail -n 30 /var/log/auth.log` (look for 'Authentication refused: bad ownership or modes').

sshd's `StrictModes` refuses to use `~/.ssh` or `authorized_keys` if they're writable by group/other (a security guard — anyone who could edit them could add their own key). Check: `ls -ld /home/deploy/.ssh` and `ls -l /home/deploy/.ssh/authorized_keys`.
Tighten the modes: `sudo chmod 700 /home/deploy/.ssh && sudo chmod 600 /home/deploy/.ssh/authorized_keys` (and make sure they're owned by `deploy`). Retry `ssh -i ~/deploy_key deploy@localhost`.
